behavior_in_task_description.reason: Tests assert on specific method name 'executeAs' (test_execute_as_method_exists, test_call_sites_updated_add_has_cdp check 'executeMethod.executeAs'), but instruction.md never names this method — it only describes 'a method that takes just a command name and parameters' without specifying the name.

no_solution_leakage.reason: Instruction describes requirements (return Object, add overloads, remove old method) without dictating exact code changes or line numbers.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests hardcode the specific name 'executeAs' (test_execute_as_method_exists regex 'default\s+<T>\s+T\s+executeAs') which is not specified in instruction.md; an alternative valid name like 'executeRequired' replacement with a different name would fail.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: All shown tests use read_file() and regex/substring checks (e.g., re.search on file content); no test compiles or executes the Java code.

test_not_tautological.reason: Tests check for specific structural changes (Object return type, removal of executeRequired, new method signatures) that a stub cannot satisfy.

behavior_in_tests.reason: Tests cover the stated requirements: return type change, new overloads, removal of old method, call site updates, imports.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are grouped with comment sections for FAIL-TO-PASS and PASS-TO-PASS with descriptive docstrings.

structured_data_schema.reason: Task is a code refactor, not structured data production.

no_typos.reason: Paths and class names appear consistent and correct.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: Instruction names the file to modify (which is the task domain) without specifying exact lines.

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: This is a code-only task, not an agentmd-edits task.
